data.text: I'll work through this bead step by step. Let me start by understanding the current state of the repository and what needs to be documented.

data.output:
1 # DDx — Document-Driven Development eXperience 2 3 [![CI](https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/actions/workflows/ci.yml) 4 [![Go Version](https://img.shields.io/github/go-mod/go-version/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx?filename=cli/go.mod)](https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx) 5 [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT) 6 7 > Documents drive the agents. DDx drives the documents. 8 9

10 11

12 13 **[Full Documentation →](https://DocumentDrivenDX.github.io/ddx/)** 14 15 ## What You Just Saw 16 17 DDx + HELIX takes a project from zero to working software: 18 19 1. `ddx init` — create a document library 20 2. `ddx install helix` — install the HELIX workflow plugin 21 3. Agent frames the project — creates PRD, feature specs, and tracker beads 22 4. Agent builds it — TDD, one commit per bead, all tests passing 23 5. Agent evolves it — adds a feature, updates specs, extends code 24 6. `ddx bead list` — every step tracked, every bead closed 25 26 ## Quick Start 27 28 ```bash 29 # Install DDx 30 curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/main/install.sh | bash 31 32 # Initialize your project 33 cd your-project 34 ddx init 35 36 # Install HELIX workflow plugin 37 ddx install helix 38 39 # Explore 40 ddx doctor 41 ddx persona list 42 ddx bead list 43 ``` 44 45 ## Pre-claim Intake and Silent-Idle Diagnosis 46 47 Before a bead is claimed, DDx runs the pre-claim intake hook and evaluates the 48 shared `readiness_checks` payload. The intake contract accepts these canonical 49 verdict forms: 50 51 - JSON bool `true` -> `pass` 52 - JSON bool `false` -> `fail` 53 - JSON strings are passed through after trimming 54 - `null` or absent -> empty 55 56 See `ClassifyReadinessWithMode` 57 (`cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go:56-115`) for the mapping from 58 readiness classifications to worker behavior. The worker treats the shared 59 schema as a read-only intake contract and classifies the verdict into one of 60 the following failure classes: 61 62 - `system_unready` / `intake_error` are hard errors from the hook, but the 63 worker fail-opens and skips the claim instead of parking the bead. 64 - `needs_refine` is warn-only in warn mode and becomes operator-attention / 65 park in block mode. 66 - `operator_required` parks the bead. 67 - `needs_split` parks the bead for decomposition. 68 69 If the worker idles on a full queue, inspect the latest 70 `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-*.jsonl` file and start with `loop.idle` events. 71 Repeated identical blocker details are escalated after 72 `preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold` cycles (`ddx-df77e668`), so a silent stall 73 with the same fingerprint should eventually become a 74 `loop.operator_attention` signal instead of looping forever. Track the exact 75 blocker detail, and distinguish `preclaim_systemic` from 76 `preclaim_tracker_contention`. 77 78 For a queue that is technically moving but still claims very slowly, use the 79 claim-success-rate warning knobs `--claim-rate-window` and 80 `--claim-rate-threshold` to surface the low-claim-rate condition. `ddx work --watch` 81 emits the warning to the operator stream once the rolling success rate over a 82 full window falls at or below the threshold, so a low-claim loop is visible 83 even when the queue is not fully idle. Use the latest agent loop log plus 84 `ddx work status` to distinguish slow progress from a silent intake failure. 85 86 **Related reliability context:** AR-2026-05-17 follow-up; lock handling 87 (ddx-57c40485); route-resolution wedge handling (ddx-8f2e0ebf). 88 89 ## Development 90 91 ### Local Install 92 93 The canonical local binary is `${HOME}/.local/bin/ddx`. Use the installer for 94 all local deployments: 95 96 ```bash 97 make build 98 ./install.sh --from-build 99 ``` 100 101 `make install` runs the same installer path after building. Avoid copying DDx 102 to other PATH directories by hand; add an installer mode when a different 103 deployment source is needed. Use `./install.sh --from-build --no-shell --prefix 104 "$TMPDIR/ddx-install-test"` for throwaway installer checks. 105 106 ### Build and Test CLI 107 108 ```bash 109 cd cli 110 make dev # Start Go development server 111 make test # Run all tests 112 ``` 113 114 ### Frontend Development (SvelteKit) 115 116 The web UI is built with SvelteKit and Bun: 117 118 ```bash 119 # Install dependencies and start dev server 120 cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun install && bun run dev 121 122 # Generate GraphQL types from schema 123 bun run houdini:generate 124 125 # Run unit tests 126 bun run test 127 128 # Run e2e tests with Playwright 129 bun run test:e2e 130 ``` 131 132 ## Build Something 133 134 ```bash 135 # Frame: create specs and work items 136 ddx run --harness claude --prompt frame-prompt.md 137 138 # Build: agent implements per specs, TDD, closes beads 139 ddx try ddx-abc12345 140 141 # Evolve: add a feature 142 ddx work 143 144 # Inspect 145 ddx bead list # all beads tracked 146 ddx log # history 147 ddx doc history PRD-001 # spec evolution 148 ddx metric list # metric artifacts 149 ddx metric show MET-001 # metric definition + recent history 150 ``` 151 152 `ddx metric` inspection commands are read-only projections over the exec 153 substrate. `ddx metric run ` is the convenience wrapper for 154 `ddx exec run ` that resolves the latest active definition 155 bound to the metric artifact. 156 157 ## Key Commands 158 159 | Command | What it does | 160 |---------|-------------| 161 | `ddx init` | Initialize document library | 162 | `ddx install ` | Install a workflow plugin | 163 | `ddx doctor` | Validate installation health | 164 | [Prose quality support](docs/prose-quality.md) | Guidance for `ddx doc prose --changed` and prose config | 165 | `ddx bead create/list/ready` | Track work items | 166 | `ddx metric list/show/validate/run/history/compare/trend` | Inspect metric artifacts and run history | 167 | `ddx run` | Invoke an AI agent once | 168 | `ddx try` | Execute a specific bead | 169 | `ddx work` | Drain the bead queue | 170 | `ddx log` | View history | 171 | `ddx persona bind` | Assign personas to roles | 172 173 ## Ecosystem 174 175 ``` 176 Workflow tools (HELIX, etc.) → opinionated practices 177 DDx (this project) → document infrastructure 178 AI agents (Claude, etc.) → consume docs, produce code 179 ``` 180 181 ## License 182 183 MIT. See [LICENSE](LICENSE). 184

13	func repoRootForReadmeContractTest(t *testing.T) string {
14		t.Helper()
15	
16		_, file, _, ok := runtime.Caller(0)
17		require.True(t, ok, "runtime.Caller failed")
18	
19		return filepath.Clean(filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(file), "..", ".."))
20	}
21	
22	func TestReadmeDocumentsPreClaimIntakeContract(t *testing.T) {
23		root := repoRootForReadmeContractTest(t)
24	
25		readmePath := filepath.Join(root, "README.md")
26		data, err := os.ReadFile(readmePath)
27		require.NoError(t, err)
28	
29		readme := string(data)
30		required := []string{
31			"Pre-claim Intake and Silent-Idle Diagnosis",
32			"readiness_checks",
33			"bool `true` -> `pass`",
34			"bool `false` -> `fail`",

49			"AR-2026-05-17",
50			"ddx-57c40485",
51			"ddx-8f2e0ebf",
52		}
53		for _, term := range required {
54			assert.Contains(t, readme, term, "README.md must document %q", term)
55		}
56	}

data.text:
Perfect! All acceptance criteria are fully satisfied:

**AC1** ✓ — README.md contains the pre-claim intake section with all required documentation:
- Readiness checks contract (verdict bool/string/null forms)
- Failure-class mapping (warn-only, operator-attention, hard errors)
- Warn-fingerprint escalation threshold and `preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold`
- `--claim-rate-window` and `--claim-rate-threshold` flags
- Silent-idle diagnosis playbook
- Test `TestReadmeDocumentsPreClaimIntakeContract` passes

**AC2** ✓ — `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green` — all tests pass

**AC3** ✓ — `lefthook run pre-commit` passes

The README.md already contained all required documentation. The test file `cli/cmd/readme_preclaim_intake_test.go` exists and verifies all 20+ required terms are present in the documentation. Work is complete.
